creator: bsd@volki.at
text:
Thank you for directing me to make.conf, where I forgot the initial "#" in a comment line. I did not expect a syntax error in make.conf to cause /usr/bin/make to be called instead of /usr/local/bin/gmake. Anyway, the error was my fault and the bug can be closed.

Just in case anyone wonders, here's the line ("SUP: obsolet...") that caused the misbehaviour:

...
USA_RESIDENT=NO
#MASTER_SITE_BACKUP?=   \
#       ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/ports/distfiles/${DIST_SUBDIR}/
SUP: obsolet, geht jetzt mittels svnlite
#SUP_UPDATE=     yes
#SUP=            /usr/local/bin/cvsup
#SUPFLAGS=       -g -L 2
#SUPFILE=        /usr/local/etc/standard-supfile
#PORTSSUPFILE=   /usr/local/etc/ports-supfile
##DOCSUPFILE=     /usr/local/etc/doc-supfile
TOP_TABLE_SIZE= 127
...
